label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking whether following these instructions will bring me back to the starting point. The instructions are: always face forward; take 2 steps backward, then 5 steps backward, then 5 steps right, then 9 steps right. The options are Yes or No. First, I need to visualize this. Let me start by assuming that when the instructions say "always face forward," that means my initial facing direction doesn't change during the process. So, I'm facing the same direction throughout all the steps. Next, I need to map out the steps. If I'm facing forward, then taking a step backward would be in the opposite direction of forward. Taking a step right would be to my right side, which is perpendicular to the forward direction. Let me define a coordinate system to track my position. Let's say that my starting point is at (0, 0). If I'm facing forward, let's define that as facing along the positive y-axis (upwards on a standard Cartesian plane). Therefore: - Facing forward means my direction is along the positive y-axis. - Taking a step backward would be in the negative y direction. - Taking a step to the right would be in the positive x direction. Now let's track my movements: 1. Start at (0, 0) 2. Take 2 steps backward: Each step is 1 unit, so 2 steps backward = -2 in the y-direction. New position: (0, -2) 3. Take 5 steps backward: Another -5 in the y-direction. New position: (0, -7) 4. Take 5 steps right: 5 in the x-direction. New position: (5, -7) 5. Take 9 steps right: Another 9 in the x-direction. New position: (14, -7) So I end up at position (14, -7), which is clearly not the same as my starting position of (0, 0). Therefore, I don't return to the starting point. Let me double-check my reasoning: - Facing forward didn't change my direction during the process, so all backward steps were in the same direction (negative y), and all right steps were in the same direction (positive x). - My total movement in the y-direction was -2 - 5 = -7 units - My total movement in the x-direction was +5 + 9 = +14 units - Final
